description: A type confusion vulnerability exists in V8's WebAssembly type canonicalization due to a transient canonical index overflow. In `TypeCanonicalizer::CanonicalizeTypeDef()` (canonical-types.cc), the `CanonicalizeTypeIndex` lambda computes a provisional absolute canonical index for types within a new recursion group by adding `canonical_recgroup_start.index` to the relative offset. If this sum exceeds `kMaxCanonicalTypes` (the maximum number of canonical types), the resulting index overflows the 20-bit `PayloadField` of `CanonicalValueType` and is silently truncated when stored via `ValueType::Canonicalize()`. The `CheckMaxCanonicalIndex()` guard is only invoked *after* confirming the group is new, so the overflow is not caught during the equality comparison phase. This causes `CanonicalEquality::EqualValueType()` to compare truncated (incorrect) indices, allowing two structurally distinct recursion groups to hash-collide and be assigned the same canonical type index. The regression was introduced by commit 44171ac which removed the `CHECK_LT()` safety check that previously existed in `CanonicalValueType::FromIndex()`. An attacker can exploit a meet-in-the-middle hash collision attack to craft a WebAssembly module where a relative reference (canonical index `0x100000 + N`) and an absolute reference (canonical index `N`) are treated as identical, enabling arbitrary type confusion between struct types and subsequent in-sandbox memory corruption primitives.

fixes.evidence_text: Local patch generated from verified V8 commit 700e9d09e4c916e7ea510b85f53e95e1f5ea2f93 for CL 6316677.
